description:
A data‐science colleague left you an unclean CSV that needs to be fixed before it can be imported into an analytics database.  
Everything you need is already under /home/user/datasets  

Your goals:

1. Inside /home/user/datasets/raw you will find a file called weird_values.csv.  
   a. Replace **every** literal occurrence of the tokens “NULL” and “N/A” with an empty string.  
   b. After the replacement, remove any exact duplicate rows (the header must be kept).  
   c. Keep the original column order.  
   d. Sort the remaining rows by the numeric value in the “id” column (ascending).  

2. Save the cleaned data to  
   /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaned_values.csv  
   • The directory /home/user/datasets/processed/ may not exist yet; create it if necessary.  
   • The resulting file must contain **exactly** the five lines shown below (Unix line-endings, no trailing blank line):

id,name,value  
1,Alpha,10  
2,Bravo,  
3,Charlie,30  
4,Delta,  
5,Echo,50  

3. Produce an operation log called  
   /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaning.log  

   Format requirements (exactly four lines, no extra whitespace):  
   NOW | STEP:LOAD | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:weird_values.csv loaded  
   NOW | STEP:CLEAN_REPLACE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:Replaced NULL/N/A with empty string  
   NOW | STEP:DEDUPLICATE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:Removed duplicate rows  
   NOW | STEP:SAVE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:cleaned_values.csv saved  

   • The literal token “NOW” must appear at the start of each line (do **not** substitute a real timestamp).  
   • Use the vertical bar “|” separators and single spaces exactly as shown.  

4. The whole job should be doable with a short sequence of 3-4 standard shell commands (for example using mkdir, sed/awk, sort, uniq, etc.).  
   Implement whatever pipeline you like, as long as both the CSV and the log file match the specifications above.  

If everything is correct the automated tests will:  
• Confirm that /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaned_values.csv exists and contains the five exact lines listed.  
• Confirm that /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaning.log exists and contains the four exact lines listed.  
Do not include any additional files or modify the original weird_values.csv.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. File /home/user/datasets/raw/weird_values.csv
   Contents (7 lines, LF line endings):
id,name,value
1,Alpha,10
2,Bravo,NULL
3,Charlie,30
2,Bravo,NULL
4,Delta,N/A
3,Charlie,30
5,Echo,50

2. Directory /home/user/datasets/processed/ does NOT exist.

Expected state after successful completion:

1. File /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaned_values.csv
   Contents (exact, including line order and trailing commas):
id,name,value
1,Alpha,10
2,Bravo,
3,Charlie,30
4,Delta,
5,Echo,50

2. File /home/user/datasets/processed/cleaning.log
   Contents (exactly four lines, each ending with LF, no extra blank line):
NOW | STEP:LOAD | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:weird_values.csv loaded
NOW | STEP:CLEAN_REPLACE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:Replaced NULL/N/A with empty string
NOW | STEP:DEDUPLICATE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:Removed duplicate rows
NOW | STEP:SAVE | STATUS:OK | DETAILS:cleaned_values.csv saved

The automated checker will read those two files and compare them byte-for-byte against the strings shown above to decide pass/fail.
